generated_text:
I’m sitting here trying to focus but the noise from the coffee shop is maddening. I can’t even think straight. Why does everyone feel the need to chat so loudly? It’s not a concert, people. Just bring your coffee and sip it quietly. Why do they insist on talking about their boring lives? “Oh, I went to the mall and bought this great dress.” Who cares? It’s like my ears are in a constant state of annoyance. Everything feels like a competition of who can be more pretentious. If only I could just tune it all out. Can’t they see that the world doesn’t revolve around their trivial conversations? 

I miss the days when people just picked up a book or sat quietly. Is that too much to ask? People are incapable of silence these days. And why is everyone glued to their phone screens? I mean, really, is that conversation worth it?  Or are they just trying to look busy? I know they can’t possibly be having a fulfilling experience scrolling through social media; it’s all curated nonsense. Imagine trying to impress someone with a filter over your face. It’s pathetic. Yet here they are, acting like everything is so fabulous. Fake smiles and staged moments. I want to shake them and say, “Do you really believe that?” But I know they don’t want to hear it. Nobody does. 

Then there are the people who insist on being overly nice, the kinds who think that a simple hello or asking how your day is going is supposed to brighten your mood. It doesn’t. I mean, who are you to assume I want to chat? Just let me be. It’s like they’re trying to spread this fake positivity like a virus, and I’m standing there, covered in their overzealous cheer. “Oh, just look on the bright side!" No. Sometimes, it’s just okay to acknowledge the dark side. Life isn’t a Hallmark movie. It’s not all warm fuzzies and happy endings. 

And don’t get me started on group projects. It’s a miracle that I haven’t thrown something. I can’t stand working with people who don’t pull their weight. It’s infuriating. Why is it always the case that I’m stuck with the ones who just want to coast through life? I can’t take it. It’s like I’m the only one who cares about getting things done, and they’re all too busy playing nice. I’m tempted to just do everything myself, but then I’m stuck redoing all their half-baked ideas anyway. It’s exhausting. 

I had a professor who said that collaboration is key to success. I rolled my eyes. Collaboration in theory is great, but in practice? It’s a nightmare. Everyone dances around each other’s feelings instead of being direct. Just say what you think, for crying out loud! I’d rather have someone say, “This idea is trash,” than to sit through a 20-minute discussion of how to pad someone’s ego. Enough with the pretense. 

And why can't people own their opinions? Everyone wants to play it safe. The political correctness is suffocating. Sometimes I think we’re all just skirting around reality, pretending that we can’t say what we actually feel. Like, when did it become taboo to actually disagree? Are we all just supposed to nod along and smile? I’d rather be honest and considered a jerk than be fake. If I don’t like something, I’ll just say it. But then I get this look, like I’m the villain. Maybe it’s just easier to be agreeable for everyone else, but come on. 

I just want to scream. Sometimes I feel like I don’t belong anywhere. It’s frustrating. It seems like everyone wants to conform to this mold of what’s acceptable. I can’t pretend. I can’t sit silently when I want to yell. And then there’s the anxiety that creeps in. The feeling of alienation, like I’m the odd one out. It doesn’t help that my thoughts spiral. Am I the only one who sees through the façade? But I just can't bring myself to blend in. 

At the end of the day, it feels like I’m screaming into the void. Everyone else is so wrapped up in their own little worlds, and I’m just here, standing on the outside looking in. Maybe I am disagreeable, but sometimes it feels like the only thing that keeps me grounded. It’s exhausting, but I guess it’s who I am, and I’ll take it over pretending to be someone I’m not.

Today was a pretty good day, I think. I mean, it had its ups and downs but overall, I felt like I was in a good mood. I woke up early, earlier than usual, and it felt nice to have that extra time to just sort of wake up gradually, you know? I made some coffee, I really like my coffee strong but not too strong, just the right amount of cream—it makes the whole experience so much nicer. If I could, I would drink coffee all day but then I think I would be really jittery and not focused, right? Balance is key. 

After coffee, I decided to go for a walk. The weather was nice, it was one of those days where everything feels fresh and new. I love how the air smells after it rains, which it did yesterday, and the grass was so green. I saw a family of geese waddling by and they’re so cute with their little goslings following behind. It’s funny how nature can be so calming. I wonder if they know how adorable they are? Do animals feel that kind of pride? I like to think they do.

Anyway, I came back home and had a class to attend. It was a bit of a boring lecture but I always try to keep myself engaged. I feel like if I’m not actively listening, then I’m wasting my time. Plus, it’s nice to see everyone working together, even if the material isn’t the most exciting. We all have each other’s backs, right? Definitely wouldn’t want to be that one person who keeps their head down and just zones out. Not my style. 

After class, my friend asked if I wanted to grab lunch with her. Of course, I said yes. I’m always down to hang out with friends. We went to this little café that just opened up on campus, and they have the best sandwiches. I think I had turkey and avocado, and it was so good! I could probably eat there every day and not get tired of it. We chatted about everything and nothing; it’s interesting what comes up in casual conversation. She mentioned she’s thinking about studying abroad next semester and I was super supportive. I think everyone should explore new places and cultures if they get the chance because it’s just such a valuable experience. 

Then we talked about finals coming up. I mean they’re kind of stressful, but it’s also a good opportunity to reflect on everything I’ve learned. I like to study in groups because it feels less isolated. Plus, you can bounce ideas off each other, which helps a lot. It’s so rewarding when the pieces click into place. I don’t want to sound too cheesy, but it’s true. 

Later, I decided to call my parents. They always love hearing from me, and I like to keep them updated on what I’m up to. They’re so supportive of my education and it makes me feel really lucky. I told them about that café and how it reminded me of our little weekends at our favorite places back home. I know they miss me, but they’re always encouraging me to enjoy this time. I appreciate that, it’s comforting. Family is everything, isn’t it? 

Then I got a text from another friend asking if I could help them with their project. Of course, I said yes. I really like collaborating, and I think it’s important to lend a hand when you can. I mean, if I were in their shoes, I would want help too. We sat over coffee again, it seems like a theme today, and we brainstormed ideas. It’s funny how much more creative you can be when you’re bouncing ideas around with someone else. I love that collaborative energy. 

Now I’m back at my desk and I have all these thoughts swirling around in my head. Sometimes I wonder if I agree with things too easily. Am I just placating people or do I genuinely enjoy being agreeable? I think there’s a fine line, right? I don't want to be a doormat or anything but I also think there’s nothing wrong with wanting others to feel good or comfortable. It's rewarding. I like the harmony, the flow of good vibes. Life's too short for conflict, I guess. 

I think that’s why I’m drawn to peaceful resolutions and mediation. It’s all about connecting and understanding where someone else is coming from. But then again, I also wonder if I’m too accommodating at times. Maybe I need to stand my ground a bit more? I don’t know, but for now, I’ll keep enjoying the little moments and being there for others. That feels right to me.

I woke up today thinking about breakfast. Oatmeal sounds good. It’s healthy, filling. I like to add some bananas and maybe a sprinkle of cinnamon. It’s a comforting routine. Routines are important. They anchor me. I think about routines in college and how some days I just can’t stick to them with all the assignments piling up. But today, I feel okay. I’m not overwhelmed. I’m in a good place mentally. 

I remember my psychology class from last semester; we discussed emotional stability. It’s interesting how some people are so reactive and others are more grounded. I sometimes envy the emotional rollercoaster people go through; it seems intense, passionate. But I also love how I can take a step back and analyze things without getting swept away in the tide of feelings. Like when my roommate had a meltdown over a bad grade. She cried and yelled, and I just sat quietly, listening, letting her vent. I feel like I was able to be there for her without losing my cool. 

I check my phone, scroll through social media, thinking about how everyone portrays their lives online. I wonder if they look at my posts and think I’m too calm, too boring. Who knows? I guess I just find joy in the simple things, like a good book or a cup of tea. I’ve been reading this fantasy series, and it’s so engrossing, like a mini-vacation every time I sneak a chapter in between classes. I love the way books can transport you to another world, make you feel everything the characters feel. But I don’t get lost in them; I enjoy them and then move on with my day.

I feel pretty content. The weather’s nice, I think I’ll go for a walk later. Maybe I’ll take my journal with me. Writing helps clear my mind, even if I don’t really have anything to say. Just the act of putting pen to paper feels good. I’ve been trying to capture my thoughts more often, to reflect on my day and my emotions and my experiences. I like to record the little things that make me smile, like when my friend surprised me with my favorite coffee. It’s those small moments. Life is made up of small moments. The big events are great too, but they come and go; it’s the everyday stuff that really adds up.

I think about my family now, how we’re all so different. My brother is the opposite of me — he’s fiery, he gets angry quickly, and he’s always passionate about something. I admire that about him, how he can dive headfirst into arguments, convictions, ideas, and not worry about the consequences. Sometimes I wonder if I’m too placid, too agreeable. But then I remember I have my own ways of standing firm. I don’t need to be loud to make my point. I can be quiet and confident, and that’s enough for me. 

I also think about friendships and how essential they are for support. My friends can get a little chaotic sometimes, but it’s a fun kind of chaos. I mean, they’re great. We balance each other out. I tend to be the calming one, and they remind me to let loose occasionally, not take life so seriously. Like that time we spontaneously decided to go to the beach at midnight just because it was a full moon. I surprised myself with how much fun I had. That’s the thing — I’m open to experiences even if I lean more towards the stable side of things. It’s about balance.

I hope to travel one day. There’s a part of me that craves adventure, but I like planning it out and knowing what to expect. I think I’d love to explore Europe — the art, the history, the food. I’m a sucker for food scenes; I love trying new things. I still remember that time I had fresh pasta in Italy. It was an emotional experience — so rich, so flavorful. Maybe that’s the thing about food; it can evoke feelings and memories that nothing else can.

I’m thinking about the future, about graduation, about what happens after. I don’t feel anxious about it. I think I trust the process. I’ve worked hard, and I have faith that things will work out as they should. I’m excited about the possibilities, even if it’s all a bit unknown. 

I feel settled. I’m okay with being me, just as I am. I don’t need to chase after emotional highs or lows. I appreciate where I am now. I like who I’m becoming. It's all about growth, right? Today is just another step along the way.

I woke up today thinking about how quiet it is. It’s always so quiet. I like that but sometimes I feel it pressing on me, making it hard to breathe, like there’s a weight in the room. I suppose I should be thankful for the silence but I can’t help but wonder if I’m missing out on something by not being more social. The thought of being around people drains me, like a battery losing its charge. I could never understand how some people thrive on interaction, the way they bounce from one conversation to another. I prefer the corners of the room, watching. Listening. It feels safer that way but is it really living? I don’t know, I think about it a lot. I want connection but I dread the steps to get there. 

Today, I had a class that’s usually lively. I just sat there, a note pad in front of me, doodling absently while the professor enthusiastically lectured. I could feel the energy in the room, the laughter and discussion, but it felt like a wave I couldn’t ride. I kept my head down, focusing on the ink bleeding through the page, spirals and lines that don’t mean much to anyone but me. I wonder if anyone noticed I was zoning out. Probably not. The world keeps moving and I get lost. 

Lunch was equally solitary. Sometimes I think, hey, maybe I’ll just sit with someone today. But then I get to the dining hall and panic chokes me, like picking a seat means committing to an interaction I’m not ready for. I spotted a table with a couple of friends from class but my feet took me to the corner table at the back, where I could see everyone and no one could see me really. I pulled out my sandwich. It’s the same as always, turkey and cheese. Simple. Predictable. I mindlessly chewed while scrolling through my phone, pretending to be interested in whatever was trending. I think it lets me blend in, like I’m part of the crowd without really being part of it. I wish I could just be comfortable saying hi when I pass someone in the hall. Just that simple gesture of acknowledgment seems monumental sometimes. 

I can hear laughter echoing down the corridor. I can’t help but feel a twinge of envy. Are they really having that much fun, or are they just pretending? Sometimes I wonder if their joy masks a deeper darkness that I can’t touch. Or maybe I’m just projecting my own fears onto them. Being alone doesn’t always feel bad—I read a lot, get lost in stories where I can be anyone but myself. I think I’d like to be one of those characters that can talk to anyone effortlessly. Would I really trade that? I don’t know. 

After lunch, I went back to my room. It’s my sanctuary. My books lined up like soldiers. They’re all waiting to transport me away from this mundane reality. I grabbed one at random, opened it, and let the words sink into my brain like a sponge. The characters are my friends. Their world is chaotic but it’s their chaos. I feel a rush of relief in knowing these journeys will take me somewhere beyond the walls of my introversion, somewhere that feels real without the pressures of social norms. 

Sometimes I think about how much I love my solitude. The quiet is a friend. It allows me to recharge, to think, to breathe without the weight of others’ expectations on my shoulders. I can sit for hours just observing my thoughts, weaving them into stories, never once thinking about how much I’m missing out on. But when I push those thoughts away, when I think about what could be beyond my comfort zone, it sends my mind racing. I don’t want to regret not experiencing life outside of my room. 

I remember the last time I hung out with friends. It ended in chaos; someone spilled a drink, someone else got upset. I retreated to an empty corner, feeling that familiar twinge of anxiety. The laughter felt like a wall I couldn’t scale. I don’t like disorder. I like patterns, predictability. And yet, I’m stuck in this paradox where I crave connection but fear the messiness of it. I want to be understood, but the idea of explaining myself to someone feels exhausting.

I wonder if I’ll always feel this way. Will I find a place where I can exist amongst people without feeling this pull to escape? Or is this just who I am now? 

And just like that, the day fades. I sit here pondering life like a bowl of spaghetti tangled up. I’m tired but there’s a flicker of hope hidden deep within me, like I’ll discover something new about myself if I just keep exploring this solitude.

Today was a whirlwind, as usual. I woke up feeling ready to conquer the world. I mean like the whole world, with people and smiles and laughter. First thing, I checked my phone, of course—tons of notifications. My friends always know when I wake up, how? It’s like they have a radar for my sleep pattern. I love it though, it makes me feel connected. I shot a quick meme back at my group chat, something ridiculous about coffee because we all know we can’t function without it. Then I jumped out of bed, tossed on the first clothes I saw which turned out to be a slightly wrinkled t-shirt and my favorite jeans, you know the ones that fit just right, and I was out the door. 

I can’t stand being alone for too long. I just feel this pull to be around people. So I headed to my favorite coffee shop, the one with the barista who knows I like extra caramel in my macchiato. He’s cute, too, but it’s mostly about the coffee. As I walked in, I was already scanning for familiar faces. There’s something about seeing a crowd that makes me feel alive. I spotted Sam and Mia at a corner table. They were in the middle of a hilarious conversation, and I couldn't help but burst in, feeling like I was joining a live show. 

“Is this a private party or can anyone join?” I said as I plopped down. They laughed and immediately invited me into the banter. They were talking about a wild night out last week, and I missed it because I had to study. Ugh, study. Who wants to talk about that? Not me! But you know, sometimes you gotta do it. They said they ended up at this party that went til 4 AM. Why do I always miss the good stuff? 

The coffee shop was alive, buzz of chatter and laughter mixing with the sound of the espresso machine. I love that noise, a kind of music all its own. I waved at a few friends who walked in, Kevin and Amy, who immediately came over to join us. More people, yes! I felt the energy ramping up, like we were all feeding off each other’s excitement. It's electric. I love being in a group, surrounded by people who are just as chatty as I am. We joked around, swapping stories, and soon we were all laughing so hard that half the shop was looking over at us. I thrive in those moments.

After the coffee shop, we decided to head to the park for some fresh air. It was sunny, which was a plus—everyone was in good spirits. I made sure to initiate a game of frisbee because who wouldn’t want to get up and run around? The adrenaline pumping as you throw and catch, yelling each other’s names, more people joining in. There’s a little thrill in competition, and I absolutely love it. I always feel like I’m at my best playing games like that. I can’t help but shout “You got this!” to everyone, but I also make sure to tease them when they miss. 

Then there was this moment, as we were all laughing and running, where I suddenly thought about how much I absolutely need this. Like, this is who I am. I love being around people, feeling the joy of their presence. It’s invigorating. I can't even imagine being alone, I would probably just talk to myself and that would get weird really fast. I mean, who would want to do that? I want to share everything! My thoughts, my day, my ridiculous ideas that pop into my head. Everybody else seems to love it too because they kept hanging around.

By late afternoon, we all ended up at this little taco truck, the kind that’s always bustling with people. I ordered way too many tacos I know I can’t finish but who cares? Tacos are better with friends. All of us in a circle, laughing about old stories from high school, some embarrassing moments that I had honestly forgotten. I love the feeling of nostalgia, bringing those memories to life again. It’s like I’m collecting moments like beads on a string, each one reminding me of a different laugh or experience.

Eventually, we ended the day with a trip to the mall just to walk around and browse. I bought a new hat that I probably don’t need but it feels like it adds to my vibe. We snapped a ton of pictures, goofy ones and serious ones, I want to remember this day forever. I live for these moments, where everything feels right and everyone’s in sync. It’s magic really, when you get to share your life with energetic people who just get it. 

I came home feeling full—of tacos, laughter, and memories. I can’t wait for tomorrow. What will we do? It’s exhilarating to think about. I just know I’ll be ready to dive right back into it. I wouldn’t trade it for anything else.

I woke up today thinking about my to-do list. The moment I opened my eyes, it hit me like a wave crashing on the shore all the things I have to finish before the week ends. I’m a planner. Always have been. I like structure, and the chaos of my unmade bed is like a small anxiety waiting to be dealt with. I should probably make my bed first. But that leads to the laundry pile sitting in the corner. If I do laundry, then I have to think about my schedule for the rest of the week, and I have a midterm in that class I never really understood. I’ve been organizing study groups, but I can’t tell if it’s helping or if just stressing me out.

I thought I could spend the day reading for class, but I also need to review my notes. There’s this nagging voice in my head reminding me not to procrastinate. It’s always there, guarding me against the temptation of scrolling through social media. I can’t. I can’t get sucked into that vortex. I need my focus. And sometimes I wonder if I’m too focused, but it’s hard to let go. My friends don’t seem to worry about things like this. They can just go out on a whim, but I need to plan my whim. Friends are good, though. I made time for a coffee catch-up yesterday. I felt so guilty taking a break, but in the end, I think it helped me. We talked about everything — our classes, future plans, and the things we’re afraid of. Fear is such a strange thing. I keep mine in a box, but it sneaks out sometimes. Like the fear of disappointing my family. They have high expectations. I want to make them proud but what does that really mean? I wonder if it’s bigger than grades or jobs or what's written in a college essay. 

My roommate is more spontaneous. She’ll just grab her things and head out for the day. I admire that about her, but it feels strange. I can’t fathom the idea of going to the beach without planning what I’d wear or what I need to bring. What if it rains? What if I don’t have sunscreen? What if I forget my water bottle? Hydration is important, but so is being responsible with time. That’s a thought that swirls never-ending in my mind. I need to balance having fun and keeping everything in check. It’s exhausting sometimes. 

Then there’s the existential crisis creeping up on me. It’s November now, and it feels like the semester just started. Time moves so quickly, especially when you’re trying to make the most of it. I think about my future a lot — where I want to be in five years, how I’ll get there, what major accomplishments I hope to have under my belt. But what if I don’t figure it out? What if I choose the wrong path? Everyone keeps telling me how important it is to find your passion, but what if I’m just not that passionate about anything? I envy people who know what they want to do. Sometimes I pretend I know, but it’s just a comfortable lie. 

I should eat something. Breakfast is crucial, but I always skip it when I’m busy. I think about oatmeal. It’s simple. I can throw some blueberries on top, maybe a sprinkle of cinnamon. Wait, I have to check if I need to go grocery shopping again. I already spent too much last week. I hate feeling unprepared when I open the fridge and there’s nothing but condiments staring back at me. I’ll get it together, but then I remember that I need to call my mom later, and I feel the weight of that expectation. It’s always nice to talk to her, but I want to tell her about good things. Last time, she asked how I was doing, and I felt like I was bringing her down by just sharing the truth. I don’t want to worry her. I should probably clean my room, too. It’s a mess, and my mind feels cluttered when my space is cluttered. I see that pile of papers on my desk — notes, handouts, things I need to file away. 

If only I could just focus. But my mind flits from task to task like a hummingbird searching for nectar. I wonder what it would feel like to let go completely for just one day, to stop worrying about everything, and just breathe. I think I could be happier that way. But then again, would I be able to sleep at night? Would I feel accomplished when I wake up? I think I like the cycle of checking off boxes. Maybe I’m addicted to it. Maybe it’s not so bad. I’ll just keep going, one step at a time. That’s all I can do. I think about organizing my day again, prioritizing my tasks, because that’s what it means to be conscientious, right? I guess I’ll get to it then. Baby steps.

Today was chaotic, I woke up late, my alarm didn’t go off, I’ve been meaning to check the settings but I didn’t, now I’m wondering if I even set it right in the first place or if I just imagined that I did because when I think about it, it’s like a haze in my mind, fuzzy like that time I forgot my keys at the coffee shop, am I losing my mind? I can’t focus, my thoughts scatter like leaves in the wind, I don't know where to grab first. I rushed to class, I always feel like everyone’s looking at me but I can’t help it, the anxious thoughts creep in like shadows. I'm so hyper-aware of every movement, every whisper, and while I'm trying to pay attention to the lecture, I also hear someone cough, a sniffle, and suddenly I’m spiraling into worries about germs and whether I should have worn a mask, are we still supposed to be cautious about that? I should start wearing one again, what if I get sick? Or what if I don’t get sick and I worry for nothing? I thought I was over this part of my life, isn’t there a whole self-help section I read once about calming my mind, mindfulness, I think that was the term, but here I am, still in this whirlwind of chaos.

After class, I tried to focus on my lunch but the cafeteria line felt like an eternity. It’s a simple task but why does it feel like too much sometimes? The smell of fries, the sound of trays clattering, someone laughing too loudly, I can never tell if they’re laughing at me. I can’t remember if I wore the same shirt yesterday, did I? I don’t think so, but who really keeps track? I should but what if I’m wrong, again. Anxiety always tickles the back of my mind like an itch I cannot scratch. I should eat something nutritious but all I want is the comfort of mac and cheese. Is that the right choice? Am I nourishing myself or just indulging, or is it both? I try to think about balance, eat balanced meals, but balance feels like a tightrope walk every single day. 

Sometimes I wonder how I’m going to survive this semester, so many assignments I can’t keep track of the due dates, I should write them down but then I think about how I’ll lose the paper or misplace my planner. And I forgot to buy a new highlighter, the one I always use when highlighting my notes, I think I left it at the library, now I’m convinced someone else has it and it’s not even a good highlighter but it feels like a part of me is missing without it. Crazy how that works, right? I’m crazy. That's what they say about people who obsess over little things, it’s a battle in my head between rational thought and the emotional turmoil that sometimes feels like a tidal wave. I want to scream but all I can do is breathe deeply and remind myself that it’s okay. It’s only a highlighter, but what if I can’t find it again? 

I checked my phone a hundred times today. Messages, emails, notifications. It’s like a constant stream of things coming in and I can’t keep up. What was I even doing before this? I should put my phone down, but what if something important comes through? What if someone needs me? I feel so tethered to it, this device, these little screens, and I don’t know if it’s helping or making it worse, I think it’s a double-edged sword. I see my friends on their stories, they seem so carefree, and here I am drowning in my head. I should reach out, but what if they don’t respond? I don’t want to impose. I wonder if they even care about what I’m going through, it feels selfish to share my worries. Should I say something when we hang out next? It’s exhausting, this push and pull of wanting to reach out but fearing rejection, am I complicating friendships because of my own neuroses? I don’t want to bring anyone down. 

I finally made it to the end of the day, and I can barely remember what happened. Did I even go to the gym I planned? No, I just sat here instead. Netflix is calling, but I also feel like I should do some reading. Am I avoiding responsibilities? Does that count as self-care? What’s the difference? I’ve got so many tabs open in my mind and my laptop. I swear I can hear them tapping on my brain, begging for closure. I should probably write a list. Or maybe not, lists can be suffocating too, they remind me of all the things I didn’t get to. I just want to shut my eyes and rest but I can’t stop thinking. It’s like a loop. I wish I could just click pause for a moment, sit with silence, but silence scares me, it means I’m left alone with my thoughts and that’s a scary place to be.

I woke up late again. My alarm didn’t go off or I turned it off in my sleep. I can never remember these little things. I grabbed a granola bar from the counter. There are too many dishes in the sink. When did I eat cereal last? I can’t even recall. I should clean it up. I mean I really should. But I have class in 30 minutes and I haven’t even showered. My hair is a mess. I’m thinking about wearing a beanie but it’s already warm outside. Ugh. Why can’t I get my act together? This is getting ridiculous. I need to buy more shampoo. Oh, and I need to call my mom. She keeps asking if I got the package she sent but I’ve been ignoring it. It’s probably just socks. I have a million socks. I lose them all the time anyway. My closet is a disaster. Clothes everywhere, shoved into drawers, on the floor, maybe some under the bed. I always say I’ll organize it but then another week goes by and it’s still a mess. Why do I have so many t-shirts? I never even wear half of them. I want to be more minimalistic. I think I’d feel better if I had less stuff. Maybe I’ll tackle that this weekend but I always say that and then I end up watching Netflix for hours instead. That new show about the chef looks good. Or was it a documentary? I can’t even keep track. The days just blend together sometimes. I need to eat healthier too, but all my groceries are rotting in the fridge. I need to throw them out. But what if I forget to buy more? Last week I bought that bag of spinach, and it went bad so quickly. Maybe I should just stop buying fresh food? But I feel guilty. I’m always at the grocery store, wandering around. I get distracted by the snacks. Those chips look good. Anyway, back to class. I bet there will be a pop quiz. I should have studied. Or at least opened the textbook. I left it in the library. Who knows when I’ll get the chance to go back. I hope my professor doesn’t call on me today. What if I just zone out? I have a tendency to do that. I’ll be staring at them, and suddenly it’s like my brain just shuts off. I feel like everyone else is so much more organized. They probably have planners or something. I tried that once. It lasted a week. Then I forgot to write things down, and it just became another thing I lost track of. Do I even know what’s due this week? I’m pretty sure there’s an essay due. It’s on that book I didn’t even finish. Great. I should really be reading right now instead of thinking about it. Maybe I’ll skim through the summary online. Or maybe just go to class and hope they talk about it. Sometimes I wonder if everyone’s just as confused as I am, or if they’re faking it. There’s always that one student that seems to have everything under control. I envy them. Do they sleep? I barely sleep. I’m constantly tired but always have this inexplicable urge to do everything late at night when I should be winding down. I forget that I need to sleep. It’s like I trick myself into thinking I can stay up and then I crash. Then I can’t wake up on time and the cycle continues. I should probably drink more water too. I keep a bottle on my desk and sometimes I go hours without taking a sip. It’s one of those reusable ones but I forget to refill it. I really should be more conscious about it. I could even set reminders on my phone. But then I’d just ignore the reminder. Everything is a mess. I can’t even keep track of my own thoughts right now. I’m jumping from one thing to another. I should write everything down but then I’d lose the list too. Maybe it’s an issue of motivation. I can be motivated but in that spur-of-the-moment way. But then it fizzles out so quickly. I always wanted to journal about my feelings and thoughts, and I bought cute notebooks for it, but they sit on my shelf gathering dust. I’ve got this constant feeling like I’m behind on everything. Like I’m failing. That’s so dramatic. I know I’m not a failure. I just... struggle? I need to find a study group. Or maybe I should just sit in a coffee shop to try to focus. Crowds have this strange way of energizing me. But then again, I could also just end up people-watching and not getting any work done. I am so scattered. I want to have a productive day but how do I start? Maybe I’ll just finish this granola bar and make it to class. One step at a time? That sounds like a plan. If only I could stick to it.

Today started off with the usual routine. Alarm went off at 6:30, and there I was, right on schedule. I had my coffee—black, just like I like it. I don’t understand how some people can drink those sugary coffee drinks. Too much sweetness ruins the whole experience. Breakfast was a simple affair, eggs and toast, nothing fancy. I read the news while I ate, scanning headlines, and the usual nonsense was there. Politics, inflation, climate change. I’ve got my opinions. Sometimes, it feels like no one else shares my views though. I don’t mean to sound pessimistic, but people today just don’t seem to value tradition or consistency anymore, and I find it unsettling. I remember when things were simpler; you knew your neighbors, you helped each other out. It’s just not like that now. And social media adds to the chaos. Everyone shouting their opinions instead of having a polite conversation. I just don’t get it. 

Classes were fine today. A lot of talking about theories in sociology, which is interesting but can get heavy quick. I like the structure though. I can follow along. Some classmates get a bit too carried away with their interpretations. It’s like they forget that there are facts, there are studies that show how things work. Sometimes I want to raise my hand and say, “Hey, that’s not how it is!” but I bite my tongue. It’s about being respectful, after all. Respect and kindness go a long way, but sometimes it feels like they’ve been thrown out the window in favor of being edgy or controversial. 

After class, met up with a few friends at our usual spot, the cafe down the street. Good coffee, good pastries. We chatted about our plans for the weekend. I enjoy those conversations; they’re predictable in a nice way. Movies, dinner, maybe a hike if the weather permits. I like knowing what to expect. What’s the fun in spontaneity? It makes me anxious. I prefer having a plan and sticking to it. I feel like it gives life a sense of direction, a path to follow. 

Then I remembered that I still need to study for the exam next week. It’s a bit daunting; the material can be complex, but I’ll get through it. I always do. I have my notes well organized in a binder—color-coded, mind you. I can’t stand clutter; it makes it harder to think. Some classmates shove everything in their backpacks and then complain about not finding anything. Just get a folder! I want to tell them. It’s not rocket science! I used to be more laid back about stuff like that, but now I find that things are just easier when you keep things in order. I guess that’s part of growing up. 

After the cafe, I went for a quick walk around campus, just to clear my mind before diving into the books. The leaves are changing; fall is finally here. It’s beautiful, but I can feel the chill in the air. I should get my thicker jacket out of the closet this week. I actually enjoy the changing seasons. There’s something comforting about knowing that the year moves in cycles. Winter means warm drinks and cozy nights in, summer means long days outdoors. I like the predictability. 

Tonight, I’ll probably do the usual: eat dinner, spend a few hours studying, and wrap up with some TV. Nothing too wild. I caught up on a few episodes of a classic sitcom I enjoy, something light. I like to keep my entertainment straightforward, nothing too heavy or confusing. Life’s complicated enough without adding more drama. Sometimes, I think people seek out those intense shows to escape reality, but I can’t relate. I’d rather have a good laugh than dive into something dark. 

I should also start thinking about my internship applications. I want to stick to something conventional—an office job might be boring to some, but to me, there’s a sense of security in it. Routine, stability, clarity. My parents instilled those values in me, and I appreciate them. Maybe I’m just old-fashioned, but I think there’s something to be said for loyalty to a job and working your way up. A steady career seems like the right path. 

As I sit here typing this, I’m reminded of how easily my thoughts can drift. It feels good to get everything out. I’m sure I’ve frustrated my professors with my conventional viewpoints, but I stand firm. There’s solace in tradition. It’s not that I’m against change; I just think it should be approached thoughtfully, cautiously. We can embrace new ideas while still holding onto what works. That balance is key, and I’m committed to finding it. I’ll take the road less turbulent, even if it often feels like swimming against the current. Why complicate things? Life is complex enough without adding chaos to it.

I woke up this morning feeling a bit groggy, not enough sleep but still ready to dive into whatever the day had in store for me. My roommate was already gone, probably off to get coffee at that little café down the street that I never seem to try. Coffee sounds good but I didn’t feel like getting out of bed. Instead, I pulled up an article about the latest art exhibit opening in town. It’s wild how art can pull you in and make you feel something you didn’t even know was there. I love that about art, the surprise of emotion. What if I went today? Then my mind drifts to the last time I went to an exhibit. I remember the colors, the textures, how it made me think about life in a different way. It’s all so fascinating, how creativity flows through us, how we express our thoughts and feelings. I think of all the artists I admire, like Frida Kahlo and her raw honesty. The way she painted her pain, her love, her identity. I wonder if I could do that—paint or create something that spills my heart onto a canvas. Not that I’m an artist but the thought is enticing.

But what if I took a pottery class instead? I’ve been wanting to try that for ages. The feel of clay on your hands, shaping something from nothing. It sounds therapeutic. I hear it’s messy and that excites me. Messiness in life is where the magic happens. I think of all the times I’ve tried something new—like that salsa dancing class last year. I was terrified to step on the dance floor, but once I did, I felt alive. The music, the rhythm, the laughter. I think I was awful, but who cares? It was about moving, feeling, being free. I want more of that.

I glance out the window and see the leaves starting to change color. Fall is creeping in. The world is full of transformations. It’s like nature is putting on a show just for us. I remember making pumpkin spice muffins the last time the air turned crisp. I always burn the first batch but the second usually turns out great. There’s something so cozy about baking in the autumn. I could invite friends over for a little get-together. A spontaneous gathering where we just talk about everything—books, life dreams, travels. And speaking of travel, I want to go to Japan someday. The culture, the food, the cherry blossoms. It feels like such a vibrant, rich experience just waiting to happen. I wonder what it would be like to wander through the streets of Kyoto, get lost, and find some hidden gem of a café. Or to visit temples, breathe in the history. Just thinking about it makes me excited.

But then I remember I have schoolwork to do. Ugh, responsibilities. I have that huge paper to write for my sociology class. The deadline is creeping up, and I’m not sure where to start. It’s always easier to think of the fun stuff than sit down and focus. But the topic is interesting, something about the impact of social media on relationships. It’s so relevant and there’s endless material to draw from. I could totally lose myself in research. I have so many thoughts on how social media connects us but also distances us at the same time. It’s like a double-edged sword. I find myself scrolling late into the night when I should be sleeping. I think about unfollowing negative accounts, curating my feed to be more positive and inspiring. It’s amazing how the digital world affects our mental health, like it’s an extension of ourselves. Do we even know who we are without our online personas?

I should probably meditate on that for a few minutes. Grounding myself is essential, especially when feeling overwhelmed. I’ve been meaning to incorporate that into my routine. I hear it helps with focus, with anxiety. I remember trying it once, sitting cross-legged on my bedroom floor, and it was hard to quiet my mind. Thoughts racing, like a hamster on a wheel. But if I keep practicing, I know it will get easier. Maybe I’ll try it later, right after this burst of thinking. I wish I could just stay in this flow forever, letting my thoughts spill out like a river.

So many ideas and dreams bouncing around in my head. Should I grab my backpack and head out, explore the art exhibit, then maybe the coffee shop? Yes, that sounds good. I should live more spontaneously and embrace these moments. Life is about the unexpected. I want to meet new people, get involved in random events, take chances. I want to mix up my routine because that’s where all the growth is, right? I can’t wait to see what the day holds. Every moment is an opportunity, a chance for something new. And who knows what I’ll learn or who I’ll meet? It’s a whole world out there, just waiting for me to jump in.

I woke up a little late today. My alarm went off but I hit snooze way too many times. It’s like my brain thinks it can negotiate with time. I rolled out of bed, my feet hitting the cold floor, and immediately regretted not putting on socks. The air has that slight chill. It doesn’t help that I keep thinking about how much I need to do today. Classes, assignments, maybe even some time at the library if I can muster the energy. I dread the thought of being in the library again, surrounded by silent people hunched over their books. It’s a weird mix of comfort and anxiety—the smell of old paper, the soft rustling of pages, but also the weight of all those expectations. I made coffee first thing because I cannot function without it. Coffee is life. The kind of life that buzzes and hums and keeps me awake despite the exhaustion that wraps around me like a heavy blanket. I forgot to eat breakfast, though. It’s just one of those things that slips away when I’m rushing. I should’ve made toast. Love toast. But now it’s just that nagging feeling in my stomach. I’ll grab something later, I tell myself, as if that makes it okay. The drive to campus was fine until I hit traffic. Typical, right? Everyone’s in a hurry, moving like they’re all racing to some invisible finish line. It frustrates me. Why are we all in such a rush? I really just wanted to enjoy the music and the quiet of the car, but noooo, of course, there’s a line of brake lights ahead of me and I’m stuck. It’s like a metaphor for life, isn’t it? Sometimes you hit a wall, and other times you just have to stop and wait. I got to class just in time. The professor was already talking about something complicated. I just nodded along, hoping to catch up. Everyone else seemed to be on the same page, and I was lost in my head, thinking about breakfast, about that weird dream I had last night involving my high school friends. Why do those dreams come back to me? I mean, I haven’t seen half of them in years. It's funny how memories sometimes just pop back up without warning. There was this one guy, Mark, we used to talk all the time. I went to his graduation party once and it was a huge deal with all the lights and balloons. And now? I don’t even know where he lives. Strange how life changes. After class, I met up with a couple of friends. We went to grab lunch—finally, a proper meal. The café was packed, as usual. The sandwiches there are amazing, but the wait can drive you insane. We sat outside; it was nice to just breathe for a minute, the chatter around us mixing with the smell of fresh food. I felt lighter even if just for a moment. They talked about plans for the weekend. Someone mentioned a party. Parties are usually not my thing, but sometimes I feel that urge to go, to just be part of something, even if it’s awkward or I don’t really know anyone. Socializing is exhausting though. I think about how nice it would be to curl up on my couch with a blanket and binge-watch a show. But then there’s that nagging feeling in the back of my head about all the work I need to get done. I should be studying, I should be reading textbooks, and preparing for the midterms. The pressure can be overwhelming. It’s hard to balance everything sometimes. I often wonder why I chose to take this major or that class. Did I really think it through? Or was it just a spur-of-the-moment decision? In the back of my mind, there’s always that fear of disappointing my parents or even myself. I want to succeed but success looks different to everyone. I walk back to my car after lunch, feeling a bit tired. I should probably start studying or at least review my notes. There’s an essay due next week and I’m just not feeling inspired. Why is that? I have ideas floating around, but when I sit down to write they dissipate like smoke. I need to get back to that place where the words flow and I don’t overthink every sentence. Sometimes I find inspiration in the most unlikely places—a random conversation, a quote I read, or even just staring out the window and daydreaming. Maybe I’ll take a walk later. Clear my head. Nature helps. I think about all the things I need to do, the responsibilities piling up like laundry I keep putting off. It can feel suffocating, but I remind myself that I just need to take it one step at a time. After all, we’re all just figuring it out as we go. That’s the beauty and the chaos of life.

I woke up today feeling a bit off, like my brain was still tangled in the dreams I had, or didn't have, just a fuzzy sense of waking up in the middle of a scene that doesn't quite feel right. I think I spent too long staring at my phone before bed scrolling through social media, watching people live their lives, or maybe it's just my mind comparing itself to the highlight reels of everyone else. I don’t know if I even care about their lives or if it just makes me feel more disconnected from the world. Sometimes I just want to scream or cry but I don’t feel like I have the energy for that so I just keep it all inside. I get dressed, it's just a simple shirt and jeans, but I think about what I wear too much like it even matters, does it? It’s like I’m trying to put on the armor of normalcy or something but it never quite fits, and what if I don’t fit in at all? The thought sends a pit into my stomach. I think I'm fine being alone, but I always have that nagging feeling that maybe I should be out there, mingling, connecting, whatever people do. I walk to the café down the street, it's a short walk, just a few blocks, but it feels like a mini expedition every time. My headphones are in, but I’m not really listening to anything, just using them as a barrier to the outside world, to block out the chatter and noise of the city. Is that odd? I mean, I love the feeling of being out among people but it’s exhausting. I watch people as they pass by, some are deep in conversation, others are laughing, just existing in their moments, and here I am, feeling like a ghost in my own life. I wonder what it’s like for them to just be so easily engaged, so naturally social, without overthinking every minute interaction. I get to the café and stand in line, the smell of coffee is overwhelming, and I crave the comfort of a warm cup but I can’t shake the anxiety of ordering. What if I stutter? What if I order the wrong thing? It’s so silly but it’s real. I finally get to the counter and mutter my order, the barista smiles but her smile feels so heavy with expectation. I don't know why I can't just respond normally. I get my drink, thank her, and sit at a table in the corner, my little nook away from everything. I take a sip, it’s warm and buttery, a little like home, and I let the caffeine settle in like an old friend who understands my weirdness. I pull out my notebook, the pages are scattered with half-formed thoughts, and doodles that probably don’t mean anything. Words and sketches flow out, they are like little breadcrumbs of my mind, a way to untangle the knots of my thoughts. I’m not sure what I’m trying to say, maybe I just need to let my thoughts spill out on the page, to empty my head of everything swirling in there. There’s a couple across from me, they’re engrossed in each other, laughing and sharing inside jokes. I can’t help but feel a bit envious of how easy it seems for them. It’s like they have this bubble of warmth around them that I can't quite reach. My eyes drop to my notebook, but I still feel like I’m eavesdropping, picking up bits of their conversation without even trying to. I wish I could be that comfortable with someone, but all I can muster is a quick text to a friend, a safe distance away from any real-life dialogue. I think about how I've conditioned myself to be this way. Is it something I was born with, or something I developed? I remember in high school, hiding in the library during lunch, feeling like I was the only one between the worlds of deep engagement and utter isolation. Now, in college, I still find solace in quiet corners, places where I can just think, where noise and chaos don’t invade, but sometimes I think I might be missing out. I have friends, sure, but social gatherings take so much out of me. Maybe it's the pressure of small talk or the fear of judgment, or maybe it's just the exhaustion that comes from trying to put on a mask of confidence when inside I feel like I’m just waiting for the moment I can escape back to my safe bubble. I look around the café, people are getting up, moving about, the energy shifts, and I realize I’ve been lost in thought for a long time. I take another sip of my coffee, it’s almost gone, and I feel a little lighter. I might be introverted, but I can still carve out my own little moments in this chaotic world.

I woke up this morning and it felt kind of nice, you know? The sun was already up and I could hear the birds, they were chirping a lot which is great, I love birds, they always seem so happy. I made breakfast, just some toast and jam, nothing fancy but I feel like it’s the little things that matter. Sometimes I think about how lucky I am to have food, some people aren’t as fortunate and I feel a pang of guilt but I try to push it aside because I can’t change the world in one day. I do what I can, like volunteering sometimes, and I’ve met so many amazing people that way. Everyone is just trying their best, you know? It makes you realize how connected we all are even if we don’t know each other personally. 

After breakfast, I thought about going for a walk but then I remembered I have that assignment due next week, and I should probably get started on that. I really want to do well, and I hope my professor likes my ideas. She always seems so supportive and encouraging, which makes it easier to put my thoughts out there. It’d be great if everyone could be like that, just positive energy all around. I tried to focus on my notes, but then I got distracted thinking about how much I love my study group. They’re great. We laugh a lot, and I like how we can disagree on things but still respect each other. That’s such an important quality to have, I think. It’s so easy to get angry or frustrated with someone who thinks differently but it’s way more productive to listen and try to understand.

I remember last week we argued about politics. It got a bit heated but we all agreed to keep it friendly and just share our viewpoints. I think it’s really cool that we can do that without it turning into a huge fight. It’s just refreshing. Sometimes I wish more people could see things that way, with empathy and openness instead of hostility. It’s funny how people can hold onto their beliefs so tightly when there’s so much variety in the world. I mean it’s like, can you imagine how boring things would be if everyone agreed on everything? It’s good to have different perspectives, it helps us grow. I wonder if I should maybe read some articles on opposing viewpoints just to broaden my own understanding. 

Anyway, after the assignment, I thought about grabbing coffee later. I usually go to that little café downtown. The barista is super friendly, he remembers my order every time! A small mocha with oat milk. It just feels nice to know someone recognizes you, like you matter in that moment. I like to think it makes his day better too. Sometimes, I’ll sit and people watch or just read a book. I like the idea that everyone has their own story, their own battles. You never know what someone’s going through, so why not be kind? 

Speaking of kindness, I saw this video the other day about random acts of kindness and it made me really happy. It’s just so uplifting. I think I want to start doing that more, like paying for someone's coffee or leaving a nice note for a stranger. It sounds small but little things can make a big difference in someone's day. I often think about the times when a simple compliment from someone made me feel appreciated. I wish I could bottle up that feeling and share it with everyone, making sure they know they’re valued and loved. 

Then I thought about my family. They’re really supportive too, always encouraging me to chase my dreams. Sometimes, I can’t believe how hard they work for me, and I just want to make them proud. I hope I can always repay their kindness somehow. I’ve tried to express how grateful I am but I wonder if it ever truly comes across. I’d love to take them on a nice vacation someday, just relax and enjoy. 

But then I got sidetracked thinking about my friends and how much fun we have when we hang out. We have this tradition now of game nights. It’s just a blast! Laughter, snacks, good vibes. I love how everyone’s so welcoming and it’s a safe space to be yourself. I think everyone should have that. Sometimes, we play against each other but it’s never competitive in a bad way. Just fun and light-hearted. There’s something special about shared laughter, it connects people. 

So anyway, I keep thinking about all these little things, and it’s weird how they all come together to paint this picture of what life could be like if we were all a bit kinder to one another. If everyone just agreed to listen a bit more and fight a little less, how much better would the world be? I don’t know, but I’m optimistic. I really believe we can make a difference, one moment at a time, one friendly conversation at a time.

So I woke up late today, like super late. Missed my first class and I was like ugh great start to the day. I had this weird dream though and I’m still trying to figure it out. Was it about a talking cat? Yeah, I think it was. The cat was giving me life advice. Like don’t get a job and just be a cat. But I need to get a job. I have bills to pay and my student loans are looming over me like this huge storm cloud but then again the cat was like “who needs money when you can nap all day?” and I’m like yes, cat, I agree but also I need to eat so yeah. I don’t even know what I’m going to have for breakfast, or if it’s too late for breakfast or if I should just skip to lunch but then I have class at 2 and I should either study or just keep scrolling through TikTok. I really should not be scrolling through TikTok but here I am watching these people do odd tricks with their pets and I can’t help but think that my life is not that interesting compared to theirs. Why can’t I learn to juggle? I bought some balls last year but they’re just sitting in my room gathering dust like my books. I really need to clean my room, it’s a mess and I can’t find anything. Socks everywhere and dishes piled up and I think I saw a banana peel under my bed. Gross. Do I have any clean clothes? I should just do laundry but that means I have to make a plan and I don’t even know where my laundry basket is. I just remembered I have a paper due next week. Is it next week? Or is it the week after? I remember getting an email about it but I didn’t read it fully because there were too many words and I was tired and I probably had a snack in my hands so I just kinda skimmed through it. I think it’s on some book I didn’t read. I keep meaning to read it but the other books I want to read keep getting in the way. Like the one about the girl who goes to space? I just started that one and now I’m like do I even like science fiction or am I just pretending because it seems cool? I don’t even know who I am anymore. I just want to binge a series but then I feel guilty for not studying but it’s like I study for like two hours and then it’s time to stop because my brain is like "hey, you’ve done enough," but then I just end up scrolling again. I really need to manage my time better. Should I write a to-do list? I love making lists but then I lose them. I swear I had a planner but I think I left it in the library and then it fell out of my bag or someone took it or whatever. Those planners are cute, though. Like I see people with those colorful stickers and write in neat handwriting but I just scribble and it looks like a toddler got a hold of my pen. I saw a meme about that the other day and I shared it but no one liked it. I wonder if my friends think I’m annoying or just weird. Probably weird. I can’t figure out how to connect with people sometimes. I mean, we hang out, we laugh, but sometimes it feels like I’m on the outside looking in. I should probably message Sarah and see if she wants to go get coffee or something. I think she’s having a rough time too. College is hard, like how are we all supposed to manage our lives while also trying to figure stuff out? Do we ever figure stuff out or is it just all a game? I’m always like what’s next? What do I want to do after this? Am I really going to be in debt forever? I never thought I’d be this stressed. I need to get a grip on my life. I really need to eat something. Should I go to the cafeteria or just grab cereal? Why is cereal such a go-to? Oh my god my phone just dinged. Who is texting me? Is it important? I’m not going to look because then I’ll get distracted. I really should find my headphones because I could really use some music right now. It calms me down but then I get distracted by what I’m listening to and I forget that I was trying to focus on something. Am I ever going to focus? Do adults actually focus? What does that even mean? I always thought focusing would be easier than this but it’s exhausting. It’s like trying to catch butterflies or something. I don’t even like butterflies. Gosh, I should stop getting sidetracked! Focus, focus. That’s all I need to do. Just focus on one thing. I got this, right? I can totally do this. Can I? I have to. Alright, let’s do this.

Today feels like one of those days where everything just flows. I woke up and made coffee, the smell waking me up more than the caffeine ever could. It’s kind of nice how routine can feel comforting, almost like a warm blanket on a chilly morning. I remember trying to find a new coffee blend, something bold but smooth at the same time. It’s funny how coffee can be a reflection of my mood, and I feel like I’m in a really good place today. I think back to a conversation I had yesterday about goals and how sometimes they feel like mountains to climb. I like the idea of setting small, achievable goals instead of overwhelming myself with the big picture. I find it’s easier that way; little victories build momentum, and today I feel like I’m riding that wave of momentum. 

I’ve been focusing a lot on my mental health lately, just little practices like journaling and meditation. I always thought meditation was for people who could sit still for hours, but I found that even a few minutes of mindfulness can change how I approach the day. Just taking the time to breathe, to notice my thoughts without judgment, feels freeing. I wonder how many people get caught up in their own heads, thinking they have to have it all figured out. I certainly don’t have everything figured out, but I’m getting more comfortable with uncertainty, which is a big deal for me. I think back to college applications and the pressure of having everything mapped out. It’s freeing to know that it’s okay to not have a solid plan. Life happens and often it’s in the detours where the real growth occurs. 

I remember my friend texting me the other day about how stressed she is about exams. I wish I could take her worries away. It’s hard to watch someone you care about struggle. I want to remind her that it’s just one moment in time, just one set of tests, and there’s so much more beyond that. I think about how we put so much pressure on ourselves, trying to live up to expectations, especially in a competitive environment like college. But what if we just decided to do our best and not worry about the outcome? That’s what I’m learning to do. I feel the pressure sometimes, but I can also step back and remember that my worth isn’t tied to grades or accolades. 

I saw a post on social media about self-care, which seems to be a buzzword lately. It made me realize that self-care looks different for everyone. For some, it’s taking a bubble bath, for others, it’s going for a run. I find joy in nature, so hiking has become my go-to. The feeling of the earth beneath my feet, the sound of leaves crunching, the colors of the trees changing with the seasons—it all grounds me. I’ve been trying to get outside more, especially with fall arriving. There’s something magical about autumn, the crisp air and the way the world transforms. I love wearing sweaters again. There’s something cozy about wrapping up in layers, just like my coffee in the morning, warm and familiar. 

Speaking of familiar, I think about the people who surround me. I feel grateful for my friends, we have this unspoken bond. We can sit in silence and it’s not awkward. We can talk for hours about everything and nothing. I had lunch with a couple of them yesterday, just grabbing sandwiches and chatting about life, future plans, and silly memories. I realize the importance of those connections, the way they anchor me. I think sometimes we forget to appreciate the little things—sharing a meal, laughing at an inside joke, or just being there for each other. 

I’m also trying to learn more about myself and what I want. I think it’s easy to get lost in the crowd, to forget our own desires and needs. I started to explore new hobbies, like painting. I’m not the best painter, but it’s satisfying. It’s a different way to express myself, something I didn’t think I needed until I tried. I feel free to make mistakes on canvas, and it’s a reminder that life is the same way. It’s about the process, not just the end result. 

Sometimes I think about the future. What will I be doing in five or ten years? I don’t know, and that’s okay. I feel like I’m finally getting to a point where I can embrace that uncertainty. I want to keep learning, keep experiencing, and keep growing. Each day is a new opportunity. It’s refreshing to think like that. Life is unfolding in front of me, and I’m excited for what’s next. This feeling of stability in my emotions is something I want to hold onto.

I woke up today feeling kind of restless, like I needed to be around people, I can’t stand being alone too long, it’s exhausting, I mean not in a bad way but just gives me this energy rush when I'm with friends or even people I barely know, like that buzz you get before going out on a Friday night, I always have to have some kind of plan, like what are we doing tonight, who’s coming, I can’t just sit in my room and binge-watch something for hours unless it’s with a group, just yesterday I went to this cute coffee shop with a friend, the energy was so high, the chatter, the barista laughing and joking with customers, the smell of fresh coffee, I love coffee but there’s something so much better about it when I’m with someone, it’s like the whole experience is amplified, sometimes I just want to ask random strangers how their day is going, but that can be weird, right? There’s always that line between friendly and intrusive, but I want to connect, people are so interesting, everybody has a story, don’t you wish you could just know everyone’s story? I really miss the spontaneous hangouts from last semester, just calling up someone and saying hey are you free, let’s grab sushi, and then sitting in that little restaurant for hours just talking about everything, the crazy stuff happening on campus, relationships, plans for the future, it’s like a whirlwind, I thrive in those conversations, it’s where I feel most alive, all this sharing and laughing, I really miss how easy it felt to just be with people, and I hope next week’s party is going to be great, I mean if people show up, I feel like sometimes people have too much going on in their lives, like with schoolwork and jobs, and it makes me worry that my friends are feeling overwhelmed, I want to help but sometimes I don’t know how, maybe I should just be more present, just listen more, but I also want to lift their spirits, I want to be that person they turn to when they’re down, I want them to look forward to hanging out, It’s all about that balance, right? I can’t stand heavy moods, that feeling when you’re in a room full of people but nobody’s really talking, everyone’s on their phones, it’s like that moment in class when the professor asks a question and nobody raises their hands, Silence! I gotta break the ice, I always have to, I just blurt out something random to spark a conversation, like hey did you see that new movie that came out, or did you hear about that ridiculous TikTok trend? Speaking of TikTok, I get so wrapped up in those creative videos, they make me laugh, I always send them to my friends, it’s like sharing a little nugget of joy, I can’t help it, but I worry sometimes that people are getting tired of my constant bursts of energy, and I don’t want to be overwhelming, but can’t help it it’s just my nature, it feels good to be enthusiastic, to lift others up, but I also love listening to my more introverted friends, they have such depth, they make me think in ways I wouldn’t usually, I might not always get the chance to connect on that deeper level, but I really value their perspectives, once we went to the art museum and they pointed out all these things I never would have noticed, it opened up my eyes, appreciate the quiet beauty in life, but I always want to bring the energy into every situation, even when I go to study sessions, I have to make sure we take breaks to talk and laugh, otherwise, it feels like we’re just in a library, all tense and stressed out, I get that studying is important, of course, but I want to make it fun, maybe I should plan a study group with snacks next time, how can you study without snacks? And I love just figuring out who has similar interests, it always leads to so many ideas for things to do together, I wonder if everyone feels this urgency to connect like I do or if it’s more of a personality thing, maybe I should ask people, I love those conversations, how do you recharge when you’re feeling drained? I think that’s fascinating, I mean some people need alone time and others need fun and laughter, it really does say a lot about how we interact with the world around us, I could talk about this for hours, but, okay, I need to go check on my friends, see if anyone’s up for a spontaneous hangout, it’s just too beautiful outside to stay cooped up and I’ve got this overflowing energy that needs to be shared!

Today is just one of those days where everything feels off. I woke up late, again. My alarm went off and I hit snooze. Who even invented snooze buttons? I mean seriously, what good are they? It’s not like I ever feel more rested after I hit that button. I just end up ten minutes more behind schedule. And I can’t even blame my roommate. They always wake up before me and are annoyingly chipper about it. Why do people have to be so cheerful in the morning? It’s just an unrealistic expectation. Coffee isn’t enough for me. Maybe I should just skip mornings entirely. Not that anyone cares, but why is everyone so obsessed with being productive at the crack of dawn? I don’t get it. 

Classes today were just a drag. I swear I’m drowning in assignments. Every professor seems to think their subject is the most important thing in the world. Like, no, I don’t care about the intricacies of Renaissance art history or the difference between micro and macroeconomics. I just want to survive. But here I am, stuck in lectures that feel like they’re six hours long. And the worst part? Everyone else seems so engaged. They laugh at jokes, nod in agreement. Are they pretending? I mean, I can’t be the only one thinking this is just all nonsense. I’d love to see a real-world application of half the stuff they teach. Half the time it feels like I’m trapped in a bubble of meaningless chatter. 

And don’t get me started on group projects. I can’t stand group projects. It’s like a punishment for existing. You get stuck with people who either do nothing or way too much and take charge. I had one group member who literally wanted to organize everything down to the color of the font. Who cares?! Just get it done! Why must everyone have these ridiculous discussions? Just listen to me! But no, they want to take their sweet time discussing what’s basically irrelevant. I’d rather just do it myself, but somehow I always end up in these groups. It's like fate is out to get me. Do I have a sign on my forehead saying “please annoy me”? 

Lunch was okay, but it’s hard to find something edible on campus. Everything is overpriced and tastes mediocre at best. And the lines? It’s like a torture test. You stand there, staring at the menu, wondering if today is the day you’ll finally try the mystery meat. Spoiler alert: it’s not. So I settled for a greasy slice of pizza, which honestly I regret now. Who thought it was a good idea to serve pizza at eleven in the morning? It should be against some sort of law. And salad bars should be mandatory. At least give us an option that doesn’t make us feel like we’re committing a crime against our bodies. 

I came back to my dorm and just plopped on my bed. I really don’t get how some people can be so social all the time. I don’t want to be bothered. It’s exhausting. My phone buzzed with texts from friends wanting to hang out. It’s not that I dislike them, but sometimes I just need space. Is it too much to ask? I just want to binge-watch my latest series in peace. And don’t even get me started on Netflix algorithms. They always suggest the absolute worst shows. I’m convinced they just want me to fail. How is it that I can watch a million crime documentaries but they still think I want to watch reality dating shows? I’d rather eat nails.

As the day dragged on, I found myself scrolling through social media, which is just a dumpster fire most of the time. I don’t understand why people feel the need to share every single detail of their lives online. Why do I care about someone’s avocado toast? It’s not impressive, it’s just avocado on bread. But everyone rushes to like it like it’s the second coming of Christ. And the influencers? Don’t get me started. With their perfect lives and “real” moments. I can see the fake smiles plastered on their faces through the screen. How does anyone fall for that? 

I wanted to escape into a book, but honestly, even that felt like a chore today. It’s like the universe is conspiring against me. So I put on some music. But every song just echoed my frustrations. I need something more edifying, something that doesn’t feel like it’s part of this ridiculous cycle. Maybe tomorrow will be better? Or will it just be another groundhog day? I bet it will be. Sometimes it feels like my brain just won’t shut off. I should probably start meditating or something, but even the thought of it makes me roll my eyes. Who has the time for that? But whatever, I guess I’ll just deal with it. Maybe I’ll go to bed early and hope tomorrow is an improvement. Or maybe I’ll just hit snooze again. Who knows?

I woke up early today. Too early really but I had that nagging feeling I needed to get a jump on my to-do list. I mean, if I don’t keep up with it I’ll just be buried in deadlines and that’s a nightmare I can’t afford right now. I made my bed right away, something about that simple act makes the whole day feel more organized. It's crazy how much a clean space affects my mind. I'm overly meticulous, I know, but I can't help it. It’s just who I am. I went through my planner, color-coded as always. I love my color-coding system. It’s like an art project that also keeps my life in check. Blue for classes, green for personal things, red for urgent stuff and oh boy there’s a lot of red this week. Two papers due and an exam, which reminds me I need to carve out time to study. I can’t let myself slip. That would be just disastrous. I thought about how much I have to read. I should read ahead, get that done because if I don’t, I will regret it later. 

I made my usual breakfast - oatmeal with some fruit but I was out of my favorite berries so they were replaced with a banana but still, it’s fine, I’m grateful I have food, I always try to remember that. I think of the people who have so much less. It’s a privilege to even be at this college let alone be able to have a bit of fruit in my oatmeal. I get lost in thought about the assignments and then the people in my group project who are so last minute and I wonder if I should send them reminders. Should I? Or is that too much? I just want to keep everything together. I’ve learned the hard way that if I don’t take charge of things I end up with a mess. It frustrates me to think some people don’t care as much. They’ll come in and act like everything’s chill while I’m internally screaming about deadlines and the organization that needs to happen. 

I have an hour before class. I should review my notes, right? But I also want to sit in quiet and just breathe. I think about mindfulness. I read somewhere that it's important to take a moment. So I do, sort of. I put my phone down, which is hard because I feel like I have to be “on” all the time. Social media is overwhelming sometimes, I can feel my heart rate pick up scrolling through. I don’t really post, just kind of lurk, but the pressure is there. Everyone’s doing something interesting, something fun and I’m just sitting here in my room with a cup of coffee and my oatmeal. I remind myself that it’s okay. I’m okay. I’m focused on my goals, my future. My future seems so hard to wrap my head around sometimes. I want to be successful, but what does that even mean? Does it mean a big job, or traveling, or just happiness, however that looks? 

I think I spend too much time overthinking these things. I wonder if everyone else does too. I head to class and I’m early, which is typical. I always want to be early. I find my usual seat. I sit, pull out my laptop, and look around. The room fills up slowly as people shuffle in. I wonder if they’re as anxious as I am or if they have it all together. What do they think of me? Probably nothing. It’s not like they’re focused on me at all. They’re in their own heads, I know that. The class starts and we dive into the lecture. I take notes meticulously. I can’t help but want everything to be perfect. My handwriting is pretty decent, but it should be better. I should practice. I should practice a lot of things. My mind spins with all these “shoulds.” 

After class, I rush to the library, a calm place where organization thrives. I love it. I hate studying at home; it’s too chaotic. I can’t get distracted. I pull out books, I love the smell of old books. I dig in and focus, really focus. Time flies by. I lose track of everything when I’m in my work zone. It’s satisfying in an intense way. I know this is my time. I can get ahead, which keeps me sane. I’m up against so much right now but I can see the finish line. The papers, the exam, they’re all steps. A path, a route to my ambitions. I feel this responsibility, this weight. I want to be the best version of me but that feels so hard sometimes. The world is always shifting so quick. When I finish studying, I feel accomplished. I think I can do this. Just take a breath. Tomorrow is another day to keep pushing forward. I’ll wake up early. I’ll make my bed. I’ll conquer.

I can’t believe I forgot to buy the groceries again. What’s wrong with me? How do I keep managing to mess this up? I mean, it’s a simple task, right? Just get the list and go but then I get anxious. I don’t know why it’s so hard to make decisions about food. What if I choose the wrong thing? What if I get the pasta but then I really wanted rice? I should just eat what I have at home but there’s not much left. Oh god, am I running low on snacks? That would be a disaster. I need my snacks. Why is everything so overwhelming? I wish I could just be one of those people who doesn’t overthink everything. I see them in the grocery store just tossing things in their cart like they don’t even care. How do they do it?
