Do they have a system? Am I missing some secret? If I ask someone about it then what if they think I’m weird? But I kind of am weird. Ugh, I don’t know. I can’t believe I spent ten minutes thinking about cereal options yesterday. It’s all too much. I should probably go for a run to clear my head but then I’ll think about how much I hate running but I can’t get into shape without it so I should suck it up and do it but it’s cold outside. I really don’t want to be cold and what if I fall on the ice? I saw someone slip last week. It was so embarrassing. I don’t want to be that person. I hate being embarrassed. It makes me feel so small. Maybe I should just do a workout video inside. But, what if I don’t follow along right? I can’t remember the last time I did one of those. What if I embarrass myself and I’m the only one here? I can’t deal with that. Am I even doing it right? I need to look in the mirror to check my form but what if I look stupid? I don’t want to stare at myself. I should stop worrying about how I look but it’s just so hard sometimes. What if my hair looks bad? What if I have something in my teeth? I hate that feeling. I hate thinking about it. Why do I care so much? I should just be confident but I don’t feel confident. Why can’t I just be chill? Chill would be nice. I would love to be chill. Maybe I should meditate? Yeah, that sounds good. But then I think about whether I’m meditating right and it’s just a cycle, isn’t it? I’m always questioning myself. It’s exhausting really. I want to take a break but my brain doesn’t let me. What if I forget something important? Like that paper due next week? I have to check that. I should check my email again. What if they sent an update? What if I forgot the details and then I fail? That would be just the worst. I can’t let that happen. I should probably make a checklist. I love a good checklist. So satisfying to cross things off. But what if I lose the checklist? I get so anxious about losing things. I have to keep everything organized. I can’t take that risk. I mean, I’ve lost my wallet like three times. I should get a tracker but that’s another thing to worry about, right? What if the tracker malfunctions? What if it gets hacked? Am I being paranoid? Probably. But, I can’t help it. I read an article about identity theft and it freaked me out. I already stress enough about money. I can’t afford to lose my identity. How would I even recover from that? It’s scary. Then there’s that whole thing about social media. I can’t keep up with it all. People are posting all this amazing stuff and I feel like I can’t match it. I haven’t gone out enough lately. I should make plans but then I stress about whether they’ll actually want to hang out with me. What if I’m not fun enough? What if the conversation runs dry? What if they really don’t like me? I need friends, but socializing makes me anxious. It’s a catch-22. I don’t want to be alone, but being with people is so much pressure. I just want to feel normal. Whatever that means. I think if I could just relax my mind for like five minutes I could figure it all out but then I start thinking about how I can’t relax. Silly, right? I hate feeling this way. It’s like I’m in a constant loop of worrying about worrying. I need to break free. I should see a therapist but what if they judge me? What if they think I'm irrational? I know I shouldn’t care but I do. I care so much. I don’t want to be a burden to anyone. It’s just hard sometimes. I wish I could just breathe and not think. Just be still. What if that’s not an option? Am I stuck like this forever?

I woke up this morning and the first thing I thought was I need to make my bed. It sets the tone for the day. A messy bed leads to a messy mind I’ve read somewhere. So I folded the sheets and arranged the pillows just so. It’s the little things that count, right? I’ve always been a planner, a to-do list kind of person. I like order, structure. Chaos is not my thing. I can’t imagine living without a plan. What would I do? Just drift? No thanks. I like knowing what’s coming next, like my coffee in the morning. Two sugars, cream. That’s my routine. 

Today I have class at 10, then lunch with my study group at noon. We’re all supposed to meet at the same cafeteria table. Nothing fancy, just a sandwich and maybe a side salad if I’m feeling healthy. I always feel pressure to eat healthy but sometimes a burger is just what a person needs. Still, I stick to the safe options—nothing too odd like kale chips or quinoa. I think people can get carried away with the whole “clean eating” thing. Moderation is key, isn’t it? I just want to feel good and not guilty about my food choices. 

After lunch, I’ll have my history lecture. I love history. It gives me a context for everything, helps me make sense of the world. I admire the orderly way events unfold, the cause and effect. There’s something comforting about knowing that people have lived through chaos and still come out the other side. I guess I’m a bit of a traditionalist. There’s value in the past, in the lessons we learn. Some people want to break free from conventions, but I think there’s a beauty in following the tried-and-true paths. Maybe it’s just my personality. I like stability and predictability.

I sometimes find myself getting anxious about social situations, especially when people want to go out to trendy bars or experiment with new foods. I stick to what I know. I mean, why choose an unknown when a classic will do? I’m all for expanding one’s horizons, but I think there’s something to be said for tradition. Like my family’s Sunday dinners. We gather around the table, same time every week. Grandma’s pot roast, mashed potatoes, and apple pie for dessert. Never fails. It’s comforting and grounding. It’s what I look forward to. 

On the topic of family, my parents are traditionalists too. They lived by a code of values and I guess I just absorbed all of that. Work hard, be responsible, do the right thing. It feels right to live by those principles. I wonder if some people view that as boring. Like when my friends go on spontaneous road trips or skip class to go to concerts. I admire their free spirit but I wouldn’t trade my stability for anything. I value a good night’s sleep too much to be out late on a school night. 

I thought about joining that club for spontaneous adventurers last semester but I hesitated. Not because I’m not open to new experiences but because it just sounded chaotic. Do I really want to deal with people who can’t stick to a schedule? I like my clubs with a structure. The debate club has rules, a set agenda. That’s my kind of environment. I can argue my points, engage with others but it’s all very contained. It makes me feel safe. 

I’ve also been thinking a lot about graduation. The future feels uncertain but I have a plan, of course. I want to get a stable job, something in finance. It makes sense. A clear career path, good benefits. I want to buy a house someday and settle down. Sure, I’ve heard a lot of people say it’s too early to think about that stuff but it feels right for me. I want to build a future, create a life that is solid and predictable. 

People talk about finding passion but is it wrong to just want a stable life? Sometimes I think I’m an oddity or maybe just a relic of the past. I watch my friends flit from job to job, trying to find themselves. I just want to find a good neighborhood to live in, a place where I can plant roots. I guess this is me—conventional, predictable, and okay with that. And there’s something comforting about being comfortable with yourself. I wouldn’t trade it for the world.

Today was a whirlwind of thoughts and experiences. Woke up late but it felt good, the sun was already high, blasting through my window like it had something to prove. I thought about skipping breakfast but then decided against it. Toast and coffee, a classic. The taste of the toast was comforting, like a warm blanket on a chilly day. I could get lost in the aroma of coffee; it’s amazing how one smell can bring about a million memories. My grandmother's kitchen, that huge old-fashioned coffee pot she had, the way she would smile when she served it. Memories can be so vivid sometimes, like they’re just waiting for the right moment to leap into the forefront of your mind. 

After breakfast, I had to rush to class. I’ve been thinking about how much I love my classes this semester. They are almost like little adventures. I have this one class that feels less like a lecture and more like a discussion. We go off on tangents and talk about the craziest things, from philosophy to pop culture references and some random memes that you wouldn’t even expect. How did we get here? One guy in the class always has something off-the-wall to say, but it sparks conversation that goes deeper than just the surface. And I love that. It feels like we’re not just learning but exploring ideas together, like we're all in this vast ocean of knowledge and we’re just trying to swim deeper. 

It was pouring rain on the way to class. Umbrellas everywhere, people rushing, but I didn’t mind getting a bit wet. I love the rain. I love how it washes away the dust and gives the world this fresh, clean smell. When it rains, I like to look at the way the droplets create these mini rivers on the sidewalks. Each drop dancing its way down, just like my thoughts, racing past but then slowing down at moments that catch my attention. It’s funny how when it rains, some people get so grumpy and others just seem to embrace it. I think I’m one of those who embraces it. 

Class was great. We discussed art, and I realized I have this insatiable curiosity about different forms of expression. What makes someone a good artist? Can anyone be an artist? I feel like everyone is an artist in some way, even if they don’t paint or draw or create music. We all create experiences and memories. In fact, I was thinking about my own creativity and how sometimes it can feel so bottled up. I want to paint but the thought of getting started is so overwhelming. I look at blank canvases and think, where do I even begin? It’s ridiculous, isn’t it? But then I remember that it’s not about the end result but enjoying the process. 

After class, I met up with some friends at a coffee shop. They were raving about this new café that just opened downtown. I love the vibe of coffee shops; they have this pulse, this energy. People typing away, deep in conversation, or just lost in their thoughts with a steaming cup in hand. We talked about silly things, shared our latest obsessions, and laughed way too loudly. I love being around people who make me feel alive, who challenge me and make me think differently. It feels like diving into an ocean of ideas — refreshing and sometimes overwhelming but always exciting. 

Then one of us suggested we take an impromptu trip to a nearby museum. Why not? Spontaneity is one of the best parts of being young. So we hopped on the bus, jamming to music, laughing, living in the moment. The museum was incredible. There’s something about art that speaks to the soul, whether it’s a painting, sculpture or installation. I got lost in a room with some abstract pieces. The colors, the emotions they evoke, it’s like each piece is a conversation waiting to be had. I want to understand the artist’s mind — what were they feeling? How did they decide on those colors? 

As we wandered through the exhibits, I felt this rush of inspiration, wanting to capture every little thing. I grabbed my sketchbook and started doodling, just letting my pen flow without thinking too much about it. It’s freeing. I realized how much I appreciate experiences like these. They fill me up, like batteries recharging. 

Now I’m back in my room, the world outside quieting down. I feel a strange restlessness mixed with contentment. I love that I can just sit here and let my mind wander. I think tomorrow I’ll try to paint, finally. I'll let the colors spill across the canvas the way my thoughts spill across the page. And who knows what else the week will bring? Life is full of surprises, and I’m here for every single one.

I woke up late today, had a weird dream that I couldn't remember too well but it felt important. I keep thinking about how dreams are so strange like they pull from different parts of our lives and mix them up like a blender. Breakfast was toast with avocado and a sprinkle of salt but I didn't have enough time so I just grabbed a banana too and stuffed it in my bag. At least I got some fruit in me. Sometimes I wonder how people can eat fancy breakfasts every day. I mean, I love pancakes and waffles and eggs but wow, it's a lot of effort. I wonder if people are just really good at meal prep or if they actually wake up early enough to enjoy their mornings. I wish I could wake up early, not just to have time for breakfast but to have that quiet time before the world wakes up. But I keep hitting snooze like it's an Olympic sport. 

Classes today were alright but the professor really liked to go off on tangents. I appreciate passion but sometimes I just want to know what I’m supposed to study for the exam. I think academic life is all about juggling all these responsibilities. Do I really need to know the history of pasta in my sociology class? Probably not. But hey, it's kind of interesting. I love how pasta has this whole story behind it but I can't focus on that when I have a paper due in two days. And deadlines just stress me out like crazy. I end up overthinking everything and then I can’t even start writing. I need to be more organized but it’s not really my style. I always feel like I’m racing against time and I think I'm definitely not alone in that. Everyone seems to have their own struggles. 

After class, I met up with my friend Sarah at the café on campus. She was telling me about her weekend plans, going to this party that I’m kind of anxious about. I don’t know if I want to go. Social anxiety is like this unwelcome friend that tags along and whispers all these negative thoughts. Everyone else seems to be having fun and I’m just there thinking about how weird I feel in social settings. It's such a drag. But then I think about how if I don’t go I’ll be missing out but then my introverted self says hey just stay in your room and binge-watch a show. I can’t even decide on what to watch. I’ve got a million shows on my list but then I feel like nothing is appealing. Why does that happen? Sometimes I can sit for hours scrolling and not pick anything. Maybe I need to try reading more. I used to love reading but now I just don’t have the patience. 

And then there’s the whole thing about relationships and dating. I feel like everyone around me is either in a relationship or trying to be in one and here I am just minding my own business. It’s not that I’m against dating or anything, but sometimes it feels like there's so much pressure to be in a couple. I don’t know. I keep thinking about how I would even meet someone in this day and age. Tinder? I tried it once but it felt so awkward swiping left and right like I was shopping for people. What a weird concept. And then my friend tells me about her dating horror stories and they’re kind of funny but also terrifying. What if something like that happens to me? I think I’d just laugh it off. 

I also need to start exercising again. I used to go running but I fell off that train when school got busy. It felt so good just to have that time outside away from screens. Nature can be so rejuvenating but I can’t seem to get myself out the door. It's so comfy to just chill on my couch and scroll through social media. Sometimes I think I should really try yoga, my friend always raves about it. Maybe it would help with my stress. But then I remember that I’m not very flexible and I don’t want to be flopping around like a fish. I really need to work on self-love too. Why is that so hard? I think we all need to cut ourselves some slack but it’s easier said than done. 

I guess I’m rambling now. So many thoughts just racing around. I wonder if everyone feels this way or if it’s just me. I know I’m not alone, but the feeling can be so isolating. It’s like being in a crowd but still feeling lonely. Maybe I should write it all down more often. Journaling could help. Or maybe I just need to let it all out in a big stream like this. Writing can be such a release. Even if it’s just a jumble of words.
